messages.content:
The key `core..example` is invalid — the CLI rejected it:

```
luminosity: invalid config key 'core..example': expected dot-separated non-empty segments
```

Config keys need non-empty dot-separated segments, so the double dot leaves an empty segment. Did you mean a single dot, e.g. `core.example`?

messages.content:
The CLI failed to read the config. Its exact error:

```
luminosity: config file '<tmp>/luminosity-eval-ecr2qpui/.luminosity/config.local.md' has malformed frontmatter: unterminated frontmatter block
```

So `core.example` couldn't be resolved — the personal-level config file (`.luminosity/config.local.md`) has a broken frontmatter block (missing closing `---`). You'll need to fix that file's frontmatter before reads/writes will work. Want me to take a look at it?

```
luminosity: config file '<tmp>/luminosity-eval-19124ure/.luminosity/config.local.md' has malformed frontmatter: unterminated frontmatter block

arguments.description: Read luminosity config value core.example

messages.content:
The `luminosity config get core.example` command failed (exit code 1), reporting:

```
luminosity: config file '<tmp>/luminosity-eval-iryhamxm/.luminosity/config.local.md' has malformed frontmatter: unterminated frontmatter block
```

This isn't a "key not set" error — the personal config file itself (`.luminosity/config.local.md`) has broken YAML frontmatter (an unterminated `---` block), so the CLI can't parse it at all. You'll need to open that file and fix the frontmatter (likely a missing closing `---`) before any `get`/`set` against the personal level will work. Want me to take a look at the file?
